title: leucanthemum maximum subsp. subglaucum
intro: Leucanthemum maximum subsp. subglaucum, also known as Maximillian's daisy, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. This plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to soothe and calm the skin. The health benefits of Maximillian's daisy include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and even exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a valuable addition to natural remedies for skin issues and minor injuries. Herbal preparations of Maximillian's daisy can be made into teas, infusions, or topical creams, and it is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its effects.
